_owner: TechBrot Inc. — Certified QuickBooks ProAdvisor team
_methodology: Two real record types. (1) page_close_rate: the observed lead-to-engagement close rate for a page type, measured internally from real leads — never modeled. (2) lead: one row per real inbound lead captured at intake, recording how they found us and whether an AI assistant recommended us (the 'How did you find us?' + 'which AI tool' intake fields). No client-identifying data is stored. The published page reports the live N and never reports a statistic on any breakdown with N<20; close rates with an unknown denominator (leads_n=null) are internal-only and are NOT published externally until a real denominator and N>=20 exist.

_schema.page_type: string — the page/intent cluster (e.g. cleanup-bookkeeping, qb-migration, proadvisor-near-me, qb-cost)
_schema.intent: string — the contact intent/funnel tag, where applicable
_schema.close_rate_pct: number|null — observed lead-to-engagement close rate (page_close_rate rows only); REAL, never modeled
_schema.leads_n: integer|null — denominator behind close_rate_pct; null if not yet recorded (stays null until real)
_schema.lead_source: string — (lead rows) one of: search, ai-assistant, referral, social, returning, other
_schema.ai_tool: string|null — (lead rows) chatgpt, claude, gemini, perplexity, copilot, other-ai, or null
_schema.converted: boolean|null — (lead rows) did the lead become a paid engagement

_note: The 4 seeded page_close_rate rows are REAL observed close rates supplied by the founder (2026-06-18) and are used INTERNALLY to prioritize internal linking and CTAs (highest converters: qb-cost 22%, cleanup-bookkeeping 18%). leads_n is null because the denominator was not recorded; per the standing rule no external statistic publishes until a real denominator and N>=20 exist. The AI-referral lead capture (intake 'lead_source'/'ai_tool' fields) begins logging when the form handler goes live at flip.
